Articles in this issue:

entry.content:
Articles in this issue:

Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: Washington Post: 30 Jul, 2022

  • What’s under attack by this court is freedom from religion
  • Forget a coup. Beware Brazil’s bottom-feeding congress.
  • Ignoring Afghan women and girls is to do the Taliban’s work for them
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Need a dose of humor in your day? Chat with Alexandra Petri.
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 30 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 30 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-30 22:45PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Hong Kong must brace for geopolitical ‘worst-case scenario’, finance chief warns, with over 150 Chinese firms at risk of US delisting
  • 100 tonnes of frozen meat smuggled into mainland China each night: Hong Kong steps up action against cross-border crime comeback

  • Chinese scientists build bird-zapping laser to guard airports
  • Member of marauding monkey gang put down by Japanese authorities, reports say
  • Coronavirus: New Zealand border reopens to the world after closing to almost all travellers in 2020
  • India’s Mukesh Ambani streams into race for online viewers as Netflix, Disney+ eye growth market
  • Australia Prime Minister Anthony Albanese plans landmark Indigenous referendum for parliamentary representation
  • Church or cult? Abe murder spotlights Unification Church and other South Korean ‘pseudo-religious’ groups

  • World Bank refuses new funding for bankrupt Sri Lanka

  • Unvaccinated Novak Djokovic still hopes to play at US Open
  • Zelensky orders mandatory evacuations in Ukraine’s Donetsk region as fighting intensifies
  • Coronavirus: Joe Biden again tests positive for Covid-19, returns to isolation
  • In race for monkeypox vaccines, experts see repeat of Covid-19 where wealthy nations have advantage
  • Thousands of Iraqi protesters storm parliament in Baghdad, set up sit-in
  • Elon Musk’s ‘gross’ antics turn owners and would-be buyers against Tesla
  • Catholic prison chaplain caught with drugs hidden in fast food intended for inmates – German report says
  • UK leadership race: Liz Truss surges ahead, wins endorsement from former rival
  • New York declares state emergency as monkeypox infections spread, WHO warns of more deaths to come in Europe
  • Pope calls Canada indigenous abuse ‘genocide’, talks of stepping aside now he’s 85
  • Ukraine war: US envoy to UN says Russia intends to dissolve Ukraine from world map
  • Ukraine war: Zelensky says prison attack that killed dozens ‘a war crime’, while Russia claims Kyiv carried out strikes
  • US House passes assault rifle ban, but bill likely doomed to fail in Senate
  • Why food portions are getting smaller: ‘shrinkflation’ at restaurants means less on your plate for the same price
  • A change from Jackie Chan and Jet Li – Beast Cops’ Gordon Chan on how directing the Hong Kong gangs vs police film was not like his previous work
  • Cannibalism with casual couplings, human body parts turned into furniture – madness is the new normal in Sayaka Murata’s short stories
  • 7 new K-dramas to watch in August 2022: Netflix’s A Model Family, If You Wish Upon Me, The Law Cafe, and more
  • Why Stranger Things’ Sadie Sink kills it on the red carpet – she embraces Chanel, Gucci, Prada and looks stunning in a blazer
  • Museum of Art and Photography in Bangalore, India, opening soon, will tell the story of Indian art in an entertaining way, founder says
  • On Malaysia’s toughest trek, through Malayan tiger’s last refuge, the rainforest of Taman Negara national park, a sweaty climb and muddy descent
  • Sandalwood: its history, medicinal uses, Buddhist connections, arrival in China, and how the trade flourished in Asia
  • Get on your bike and ride: top 5 ultra-cycling races for amateurs – from mountains of Kyrgyzstan to wilds of South Africa
  • What is your psychological age? Free online test from Hong Kong-based longevity firm gives answers and tips
  • Donald Trump’s 4 biggest tantrums: Piers Morgan said he behaved like The Godfather, Sacha Baron Cohen was cut off mid-interview, and a former White House aide even said he smashed plates
  • 10 celebrities estranged from their kids: Elon Musk’s daughter changed her name, Caitlyn Jenner doesn’t keep up with the Kardashians, Suri doesn’t see Tom Cruise and Maddox doesn’t speak to Brad Pitt
  • Who will get to own the newly discovered Lulo Rose diamond? The 170-carat pink gem is such a rare find, the likes of Louis Vuitton or Graff could snap it up for US$100 million at auction
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

entry.content:

entry.title: Financial Times: 30 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:

  • China agrees landmark debt relief deal for Zambia

entry.title: NY Times (Print): 30 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
